title: Quick Wins
subtitle: Maximum performance impact with minimal effort — can be implemented immediately without release risk.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → Data Storage & API Usage reveals which objects hold the most records and where unselective queries are running.

recommendations.title: Custom Indexes on High-Volume Objects
recommendations.body: For objects with >100k records: identify the most frequently used filter fields in SOQL queries and reports. Open a Salesforce Support case to request Custom Indexes on these fields. This eliminates full table scans, makes filters selective, and dramatically reduces DB CPU Time. Verify selectivity with the Query Plan tool in Developer Console before filing the case.

recommendations.title: Enable Skinny Tables for Large Objects
recommendations.body: For objects with >1M records that are frequently queried in reports and list views: file a Salesforce Support case to enable Skinny Tables. They eliminate joins between standard and custom fields and can improve read performance by 10x. Keep Skinny Tables narrow — maximum 100 columns — and remember they're rebuilt asynchronously after schema changes.

recommendations.title: Consolidate Sharing Rules and OWD
recommendations.body: Audit existing Sharing Rules for redundant or overlapping entries. Consolidate using criteria-based sharing with hierarchies where possible. Review Organization-Wide Defaults: if most records are public anyway, a more open OWD drastically reduces share row recalculation time and improves DML performance on shared objects.

title: Prioritize
subtitle: High performance impact with moderate effort — schedule for the next sprint or release cycle.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → Apex Executions & Concurrent Requests show how close you are to Apex Governor Limits and where concurrency bottlenecks exist.

recommendations.title: Apex CPU Hotspot Optimization
recommendations.body: Identify the top 5 Apex transactions by CPU time using Debug Logs and ApexGuru insights (free via Scale Center for UE/Signature orgs). Common causes: nested loops, unselective SOQL in Triggers, and unnecessary String concatenation. Use Limits.getCpuTime() for in-transaction profiling. For heavy computations, offload to Queueable Apex with chaining (up to 50 chained jobs per transaction).

recommendations.title: Implement One Trigger Per Object with Handler Pattern
recommendations.body: Consolidate all logic for each SObject into a single trigger delegating to a Trigger Handler class. Use a framework like Trigger Actions Framework (metadata-driven) or fflib-apex-common for enterprise-grade structure. Benefits: deterministic execution order, prevention of recursion, clear separation of concerns, and easier bulkification. Ensure all SOQL and DML is outside loops — design methods to accept and return collections (List, Map, Set).

recommendations.title: Row Lock Analysis and Mitigation
recommendations.body: Row Locks occur when parallel DML operations target the same records — especially common during data loads against parent-child hierarchies. Use Scale Center's Row Lock investigation to identify affected objects and entry points. Mitigation: sort batch input by parent Id to prevent lock escalation, offload to Queueable Apex with ordered processing, and avoid recursive triggers that amplify contention. Consider Change Data Capture for downstream systems instead of synchronous DML chains.

title: Strategic
subtitle: High performance impact but significant effort required — plan as a strategic initiative with a clear business case.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → cross-metric trends (login growth, storage growth, API usage trajectory) indicate whether a strategic architectural change is needed.

recommendations.title: Adopt Data Cloud (D360) for Unified Customer Profiles
recommendations.body: For orgs struggling with fragmented customer data across Sales, Service, Marketing, and external systems: implement Salesforce Data Cloud (D360). It unifies profiles through Identity Resolution, provides Calculated and Streaming Insights, and is the foundation for Agentforce. Use Data Spaces to separate brand, region, or business unit data. Prefer a single Home Org with Companion Orgs (Data Cloud One) over multiple Data Cloud instances. Critical: plan for Data Cloud access even if not immediately needed — it's an Agentforce prerequisite.

recommendations.title: Zero Copy Federation with External Data Warehouses
recommendations.body: For high-volume historical data (>10M records) or data sovereignty requirements: federate Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, or Redshift into Data Cloud via Zero Copy — no duplication, no ETL pipelines. Use Live Query for operational dashboards, Accelerated Query (caching) for frequent access patterns, and File Federation for petabyte-scale ML/AI workloads. Keep source systems in the same cloud region as Data Cloud. This eliminates Salesforce storage limits and keeps data governance in the source system.

recommendations.title: Adopt Event-Driven Architecture with CDC and Platform Events
recommendations.body: For orgs with high inter-system coupling and synchronous callout chains: introduce Change Data Capture (CDC) for near-real-time data replication and Platform Events for custom business events. This decouples systems, reduces synchronous callouts, improves scalability under load, and makes the architecture resilient to downstream failures. Use Pub/Sub API for efficient subscription from external consumers instead of CometD.

recommendations.title: Org Split or Multi-Org Rationalization
recommendations.body: For fundamental scalability challenges where the org has reached structural platform limits (metadata complexity, data storage, concurrent Apex execution, governance friction between business units): evaluate Org Split by business unit, region, or brand. Conversely, for organizations with multiple orgs that evolved organically without strategy: evaluate consolidation. Both directions are high-effort multi-year initiatives requiring executive sponsorship, careful data migration, integration redesign, and change management. Important distinction: Data Cloud does NOT resolve operational scalability issues of a single overloaded org — it addresses data fragmentation across multiple orgs by unifying profiles. Use Data Cloud when you need a unified customer view, not as a substitute for architectural remediation in the operational org itself.

title: Take Along
subtitle: Moderate impact with low effort — include in your next deployment when you're already working in the area.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → API Usage shows callout volume and which Connected Apps generate the most requests.

recommendations.title: Migrate Imperative Apex Calls to Wire Adapters
recommendations.body: In LWCs, replace imperative Apex calls with @wire decorators on cacheable Apex methods. Wire adapters enable automatic caching via Lightning Data Service, reactive updates on record changes, and eliminate duplicate server round-trips. For complex queries across multiple objects, prefer the GraphQL Wire Adapter (lightning/graphql) over custom Apex — it provides precise field selection, eliminates over-fetching, and reduces field dependencies in the cache.

recommendations.title: Improve Callout Error Handling with Named Credentials
recommendations.body: Replace hardcoded endpoints and credentials with Named Credentials (prefer External Credentials + Named Credentials, the modern split introduced in Winter '23). Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for transient failures. Log failed callouts to a Custom Object or Platform Event for monitoring, and create alerts for sustained error patterns. For high-volume integrations, migrate from synchronous callouts to Platform Events with asynchronous consumers.

recommendations.title: Audit and Consolidate Flows, Retire Process Builder
recommendations.body: Process Builder has been retired — migrate remaining Process Builder automations to Flow immediately. Then audit all Flows: multiple record-triggered Flows on the same object multiply DML evaluations on every save. Consolidate into a single Before-Save Flow per object where possible, and move heavy logic to Apex where Flow becomes inefficient (especially loops over large collections).

title: Evaluate
subtitle: Moderate impact with moderate effort — conduct a detailed evaluation before committing resources.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → Login Metrics & Failed Logins reveal authentication issues and potentially unauthorized access attempts.

recommendations.title: Security Audit: Upgrade to WITH USER_MODE
recommendations.body: Audit Apex SOQL and DML statements for modern security enforcement. Migrate from WITH SECURITY_ENFORCED to WITH USER_MODE (API 58.0+) — it enforces CRUD, FLS, and sharing rules in a single keyword. For DML, use Database.insert(records, AccessLevel.USER_MODE). For graceful degradation when fields are partially inaccessible, use Security.stripInaccessible() which strips fields instead of throwing exceptions. This is the Salesforce-recommended pattern as of Spring '23 and beyond.

recommendations.title: Login Failure Root Cause Analysis
recommendations.body: Investigate login failure causes: locked accounts, misconfigured password policies, SSO configuration errors, IP range restrictions, or expired OAuth tokens. Use Login History and Event Monitoring (Login Event, Login As Event) for forensic analysis. Sustained failed logins often indicate misconfigured Connected Apps, expired certificates in SAML/OIDC flows, or credential stuffing attacks — the latter requires MFA enforcement and IP allowlisting.

recommendations.title: Integration Pattern Review
recommendations.body: For recurring callout errors or performance degradation: review whether the correct integration pattern is in use. Synchronous Request-Reply is often misused where Fire-and-Forget or Batch would be more resilient. For high-volume real-time scenarios, migrate to Platform Events or Change Data Capture with MuleSoft/Pub/Sub API as the external consumer. Salesforce's integration architecture reference (MuleSoft + Platform APIs + Data Cloud) is now the recommended approach for enterprise integrations.

title: Weigh Up
subtitle: Moderate impact with high effort — only pursue with a clear ROI justification and stakeholder alignment.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → Concurrent UI Errors indicate frontend performance issues that may point to deeper architectural concerns.

recommendations.title: Adopt GraphQL Wire Adapter for Data-Heavy LWCs
recommendations.body: Replace imperative Apex calls and multiple LDS wire adapters with the GraphQL Wire Adapter (lightning/graphql). Benefits: precise field selection eliminates over-fetching, multiple related objects in one query, rich filtering and pagination via cursor-based predicates, and reduced cache invalidation. Use variables for reactivity (not string interpolation), mark optional fields with @optional for FLS-graceful degradation, and avoid totalCount on large queries — it forces a full record scan. For mutations, continue using LDS or imperative Apex (GraphQL mutations are not yet supported on the platform).

recommendations.title: Lightning Web Components Performance Audit
recommendations.body: Analyze Lightning Web Components for unnecessary re-renders, missing lazy loading, and excessive wire adapters. Use Chrome DevTools Performance tab and the Lightning Inspector browser extension to identify render bottlenecks. Split components exceeding 300 lines of JavaScript into child components (Single Responsibility). Avoid @track on primitives (reactive by default), prefer base components over custom implementations, and verify LWS compatibility for all third-party libraries loaded via platformResourceLoader.

recommendations.title: Aura to LWC Migration
recommendations.body: Identify performance-critical Aura Components and plan migration to Lightning Web Components. Measured results show 2-60% improvement in Experience Page Time (EPT) after migration, driven by LWC's lighter framework overhead and closer-to-native Web Component model. Prioritize components on frequently accessed pages (home pages, record pages for high-volume objects) and those with heavy data binding. Use the Aura-to-LWC Migration Tool and incremental refactoring via Lightning Message Service for hybrid coexistence during migration.

title: Opportunistic
subtitle: Low impact with low effort — pick up opportunistically when you're already working in the area.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center → API Usage Trends show whether API consumption can be optimized.

recommendations.title: Clean Up Unused Custom Fields and Metadata
recommendations.body: Identify Custom Fields with zero data population using the Field Usage report, Salesforce Optimizer, or third-party metadata analysis tools. Delete or archive unused fields to reduce object complexity, improve developer experience, speed up describe calls, and simplify LWC data contracts. Equally important: remove unused Custom Objects, Custom Labels, and inactive Validation Rules — they all add cognitive overhead and slow metadata operations.

recommendations.title: Optimize Debug Log Levels in Production
recommendations.body: Ensure no Debug Logs with Fine or Finest log levels are active in production. Elevated log levels can cause up to 10% CPU overhead per transaction and contribute to Apex CPU Time limit exceptions under load. Audit active Trace Flags and expire unused ones. For ongoing observability, prefer Event Monitoring (via Event Log Files) or Platform Events with custom observability endpoints over ad-hoc Debug Logs.

title: Defer
subtitle: Low impact with moderate effort — add to the backlog but do not actively prioritize.
scale_center_hint: Scale Center shows no urgent signals for these items — they stem from best-practice reviews and long-term governance goals.

recommendations.title: Increase Apex Test Coverage with Meaningful Assertions
recommendations.body: Identify Apex classes with less than 75% code coverage and create meaningful unit tests. Focus on testing business logic and edge cases, not just raising the coverage percentage — Salesforce no longer recommends chasing 100% coverage at the expense of test quality. Use Test.startTest()/Test.stopTest() to isolate Governor Limit consumption, @TestSetup for reusable test data, and Stub API for mocking callouts and dependencies. Avoid SeeAllData=true — always create test data within tests.

recommendations.title: Migrate from Profiles to Permission Set Groups
recommendations.body: Salesforce has announced deprecation of Profile-based permissions in favor of Permission Sets and Permission Set Groups. Start migrating: leave Profiles as minimal baseline (just license and default record type), and grant all additional permissions via Permission Set Groups organized by persona (e.g., Sales_Rep_Group, Service_Agent_Group). Use Muting Permission Sets to remove permissions within a group when needed. This improves governance, simplifies auditing, and aligns with Salesforce's long-term direction.

title: Skip
subtitle: Low impact with high effort — a deliberate decision not to pursue these measures. Document the rationale.
scale_center_hint: These recommendations appear in best-practice guides but deliver insufficient ROI in the context of your specific org's performance profile.

recommendations.title: Complete Apex Layer Rewrite
recommendations.body: A full rewrite of all Apex classes to a new framework (e.g., Enterprise Design Patterns from scratch) sounds appealing but is almost never economically justified in production orgs with mature, tested codebases. Prefer incremental refactoring of hotspots identified via ApexGuru and Scale Center. Apply Enterprise Patterns (Service Layer, Selector Layer, Domain Layer) selectively to new modules, not retroactively to the entire codebase.

recommendations.title: Custom UI Replacing Standard Features
recommendations.body: Building custom Lightning Web Components to replace standard features (List Views, Related Lists, Reports, Dashboards) creates significant ongoing maintenance burden. The effort to keep parity with Salesforce's evolving standard features — new base components, SLDS updates, Dynamic Forms, Dynamic Related Lists — almost always exceeds the benefit. Extend standard functionality with Quick Actions, Flow, or focused LWCs on record pages instead of replacing it wholesale.
